properties.name: app.cors.allowed-origins
properties.description: Allowed origins for CORS requests. This property should be a comma-separated list of allowed origins.

properties.name: api.security.token.secret
properties.description: Secret key used for signing and verifying JWT tokens. This property should be set to a secure value.

properties.name: spring.mail.host
properties.description: SMTP server host address for sending emails (e.g., smtp.gmail.com, mail.example.com)

properties.name: spring.mail.port
properties.description: SMTP server port number for sending emails (e.g., 587 for TLS, 465 for SSL, 25 for plain text)

properties.name: spring.mail.username
properties.description: Username for authentication with the SMTP server when sending mail.

properties.name: spring.mail.password
properties.description: Password used to authenticate with the SMTP server when sending email. Keep this value secure and do not expose it in version control.

properties.name: spring.mail.properties.mail.smtp.auth
properties.description: Enable SMTP authentication for the mail server. Set to true when the SMTP server requires username/password login.

properties.name: spring.mail.properties.mail.smtp.starttls.enable
properties.description: Enable STARTTLS encryption for SMTP connections. Set to true to use TLS if the mail server supports it for secure email transport.

properties.name: gemini.api.key
properties.description: API key used to authenticate requests to the Gemini service. Provide a valid key to enable secure, authorized access.

properties.name: gemini.api.url
properties.description: Base URL for the Gemini API endpoint. Set this to the service URL used by the application to communicate with Gemini.
